example-workflows.overview: Complete end-to-end examples of using the DISH SDLC mode to implement features based on Jira user stories. These examples demonstrate the full workflow from fetching requirements to completing implementation.

example.name: implementing-payment-feature
example.scenario: User asks: "Work on KAN-2" or "Implement the payment feature from KAN-2"

jira-story.summary: Add support for multiple payment methods
jira-story.description: As a customer, I want to be able to pay using credit card, PayPal, or bank transfer so that I have flexibility in how I complete my purchase.
jira-story.acceptance-criteria: - [ ] System accepts credit card payments - [ ] System accepts PayPal payments - [ ] System accepts bank transfer payments - [ ] Payment method is validated before processing - [ ] Failed payments are handled gracefully - [ ] Payment confirmation is sent to customer - [ ] Automated deployment required after SonarQube quality gate passes - [ ] Target OpenShift namespace: lab-kan2-ab

step.action: Use MCP Jira tools to retrieve KAN-2
step.response-to-user:
I'll fetch the details for issue KAN-2 from Jira.
[Using MCP Jira tools to fetch issue KAN-2...]
Found issue KAN-2: "Add support for multiple payment methods"
**Summary:** Add support for multiple payment methods
**Description:** As a customer, I want to be able to pay using credit card, PayPal, or bank transfer so that I have flexibility in how I complete my purchase.
**Acceptance Criteria:** - System accepts credit card payments - System accepts PayPal payments - System accepts bank transfer payments - Payment method is validated before processing - Failed payments are handled gracefully - Payment confirmation is sent to customer - Automated deployment required after SonarQube quality gate passes - Target OpenShift namespace: lab-kan2-ab
I'll now analyze the current codebase to understand the existing payment implementation and plan the changes needed.

tools-used.tool: list_code_definition_names - Get overview of payment classes
read_file - Examine PaymentService and related classes

step.findings: Current implementation: - PaymentService.java exists with basic payment processing - Payment.java is a base class - Only CreditCardPayment.java is implemented - PaymentController.java handles payment endpoints - Need to add PayPalPayment and BankTransferPayment classes - Need to update PaymentService to handle multiple payment types

step.action: Create implementation plan based on analysis
step.plan: Implementation Plan: 1. Create PayPalPayment.java class extending Payment 2. Create BankTransferPayment.java class extending Payment 3. Update PaymentService to handle all payment types 4. Add validation for each payment method 5. Implement error handling for failed payments 6. Add payment confirmation logic 7. Create unit tests for new payment types 8. Update PaymentController if needed 9. Deploy to OpenShift namespace lab-kan2-ab after the SonarQube quality gate passes

change.description: New class for bank transfer payments
Add support for new payment types

step.action: Deploy through the OpenShift MCP server after quality verification

checks.check: Namespace lab-kan2-ab is allowed by the OpenShift MCP server
Route URL is available and healthy

step.jira-comment:
Implementation complete for KAN-2.
Changes made: - Added PayPalPayment class with email validation - Added BankTransferPayment class with account number validation - Updated PaymentService to handle all three payment types - Implemented validation for each payment method - Added error handling for failed payments - Implemented payment confirmation logic - Created comprehensive unit tests (95% coverage)
All acceptance criteria met: ✓ System accepts credit card payments ✓ System accepts PayPal payments ✓ System accepts bank transfer payments ✓ Payment method is validated before processing ✓ Failed payments are handled gracefully ✓ Payment confirmation is sent to customer
SonarQube analysis: Quality gate PASSED - 0 bugs - 0 vulnerabilities - Code coverage: 95% - Maintainability rating: A
Deployment: - Automated deployment executed through the OpenShift MCP server - Namespace: lab-kan2-ab - Route URL: https://payment-service-lab-kan2-ab.apps.<cluster-domain>
Ready for code review.

step.completion-message:
Implementation complete for KAN-2: "Add support for multiple payment methods"
**Changes Made:** - Created PayPalPayment class with email validation - Created BankTransferPayment class with account validation - Updated PaymentService to support all payment types - Added comprehensive error handling - Implemented payment confirmation logic
**Testing:** - All unit tests passing (95% coverage) - Integration tests verified - Edge cases tested
**Quality:** - SonarQube quality gate: PASSED - 0 bugs, 0 vulnerabilities - Maintainability rating: A
**Jira Updated:** - Added implementation summary - Status changed to "Code Review"
**Deployment:** - Deployed automatically to namespace `lab-kan2-ab` - Route verified and shared back to Jira
All acceptance criteria have been met and the feature is ready for review.

example.name: modernizing-java-version
example.scenario: User asks: "Modernize the application to Java 21" or "Upgrade from Java 8 to Java 21"

step.action: Analyze current Java version and dependencies

checks.check: Review code for Java 8 specific patterns
Identify high-risk compatibility areas: domain models, enums, repository null handling, parse methods, and boolean helper methods

step.plan: Modernization Plan: 1. Update pom.xml to Java 21 2. Update Spring Boot to 3.x if required by the migration target 3. Replace deprecated APIs where safe - Date/Calendar → LocalDate/LocalDateTime - Anonymous classes → Lambda expressions - Verbose loops → Stream API where readability improves 4. Adopt Java 21 features only where they do not alter tested behavior - Records for truly immutable domain/data classes - Switch expressions where semantics remain identical - Text blocks for multiline literals - Pattern matching where clarity improves and behavior is unchanged 5. Review existing unit tests before changing domain models, enums, repositories, and parsing logic 6. Preserve public behavior unless the Jira story or user explicitly requires change 7. Run focused tests after each high-risk refactor 8. Run full test suite and fix compatibility issues 9. Run SonarQube analysis

step.action: Create a compatibility checklist before refactoring domain models, enums, repositories, or parsing methods

checks.check: Review existing unit tests for expected exception behavior and null handling
Review enum values, descriptions, and parse methods such as fromString(String)
Review helper methods such as isCompleted(), isActive(), isExpired(), or status predicates
Review whether constructors or setters currently allow invalid or null state for negative tests
Review repository and service methods for legacy null-handling contracts
Document any behavior that must remain exactly unchanged

changes.change: Update pom.xml Java version and dependencies
Replace Date with LocalDateTime in model classes where behavior remains equivalent
Convert anonymous classes to lambdas where behavior is unchanged
Refactor loops to Stream API only when clarity improves and semantics remain identical
Convert only truly immutable classes to records, while preserving legacy getters and test-facing API contracts
Use switch expressions only when result semantics remain exactly the same
Do not change enum descriptions, parser behavior, helper semantics, or null handling unless explicitly required

validation-steps.step: Run focused tests for each modified model, enum, repository, parser, or helper method
Investigate failures for compatibility regressions before making further changes

example.name: fixing-sonarqube-issues
example.scenario: User asks: "Fix the SonarQube issues" or "Address code quality problems"

step.action: Use MCP SonarQube tools to get analysis results

fix.solution: Replace string concatenation with parameterized query
Add unit tests for uncovered methods

example.name: implementing-from-epic
example.scenario: User asks: "Work on the payment epic" or "Implement stories from EPIC-1"

step.action: Use MCP Jira tools to get epic and related stories

information.item: List of stories in the epic

step.action: Determine which story to work on

criteria.criterion: Story status (To Do vs In Progress)

step.action: Follow standard workflow for the selected story

updates.update: Identify next story to work on

example.name: handling-bugfix
example.scenario: User asks: "Fix bug KAN-5" or "Debug the payment processing issue"

step.action: Identify the source of the bug

techniques.technique: Review error logs and stack traces
Use debugger to step through code

step.action: Fix the bug and prevent regression

pattern.name: starting-work
pattern.bot-response:
I'll fetch the details for issue KAN-2 from Jira and begin implementation.
[Fetching issue KAN-2...]
Found: "Add support for multiple payment methods"... I'll analyze the current codebase and implement the required changes according to the acceptance criteria. I'll keep you updated on progress.

pattern.name: progress-update
pattern.bot-message:
Progress update on KAN-2:
✓ Analyzed existing payment implementation ✓ Created PayPalPayment class ✓ Created BankTransferPayment class ⏳ Currently updating PaymentService to handle new payment types ⏳ Next: Add validation and error handling

pattern.name: asking-for-clarification
pattern.bot-message:
I need clarification on the acceptance criteria for KAN-2:
The story mentions "Failed payments are handled gracefully" but doesn't specify the exact behavior. Should failed payments:
1. Retry automatically (how many times?) 2. Send notification to customer immediately 3. Log for manual review 4. All of the above
Please clarify so I can implement the correct behavior.

pattern.name: reporting-blocker
pattern.bot-message:
Blocker encountered on KAN-2:
The PayPal API credentials are not configured in the environment. I need: - PayPal Client ID - PayPal Client Secret - PayPal API endpoint (sandbox or production)
I've added a comment to the Jira issue requesting this information. I can continue with the other payment methods while waiting for the credentials.

pattern.name: completion
pattern.bot-message:
Implementation complete for KAN-2: "Add support for multiple payment methods"
**Summary:** - Added PayPal and bank transfer payment support - Implemented validation for all payment types - Added comprehensive error handling - Created unit tests (95% coverage) - SonarQube quality gate: PASSED
**All acceptance criteria met:** ✓ System accepts credit card payments ✓ System accepts PayPal payments ✓ System accepts bank transfer payments ✓ Payment method is validated before processing ✓ Failed payments are handled gracefully ✓ Payment confirmation is sent to customer
**Jira updated:** - Status: Code Review - Comment added with implementation details
Ready for review!

tip.title: Always Start with Jira
tip.description: Fetch the issue details first to ensure you understand requirements before analyzing code or making changes.

tip.title: Communicate Progress
tip.description: Keep the user informed of progress, especially for long-running tasks. Update Jira regularly so the team knows the status.

tip.title: Ask When Unclear
tip.description: If requirements are ambiguous or missing information, ask for clarification rather than making assumptions.

tip.title: Test Thoroughly
tip.description: Don't just implement the happy path. Test edge cases, error conditions, and ensure good test coverage.

tip.title: Preserve Compatibility During Modernization
tip.description: When modernizing Java versions or converting classes to records, preserve existing public behavior unless requirements explicitly call for change. Existing tests are the compatibility baseline.

tip.title: Run Quality Checks
tip.description: Always run SonarQube analysis before marking work complete. Address critical issues before requesting review.

tip.title: Document Decisions
tip.description: Document important technical decisions in code comments and Jira. Future developers will thank you.

tip.title: Follow the Workflow
tip.description: Follow the established workflow: Fetch → Analyze → Plan → Implement → Test → Quality Check → Update Jira → Complete
